channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Try these tips to help your parents stay safe online
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: Smart windows take a page from nature's pinecone playbook
item.description: An innovative new window system keeps your home comfortable without using a single watt of electricity and could change the way we think about energy efficiency in buildings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Google scraps its diversity hiring goals as it complies with Trump's new government contractor rules
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google is scrapping some of its diversity hiring targets, joining a lengthening list of U.S. companies that have abandoned or scaled back their diversity, equity and inclusion programs.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Man jailed over police AI program, then freed 17 months after victim raised doubts
item.description: A grainy surveillance photo of an assault suspect on St. Louis' Metrolink led a computer facial recognition program to identify Chris Gatlin.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Malicious package found in the Go ecosystem
item.description: A malicious typosquat package has been found in the Go language ecosystem. The package, which contains a backdoor to enable remote code execution, was discovered by researchers at the application security company Socket.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Kyndryl expands Palo Alto deal to offer managed SASE service
item.description: Kyndryl has expanded its alliance with Palo Alto Networks to add secure access service edge (SASE) services to its managed services offerings.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nikon's first 35mm f/1.2 looks like my dream prime lens – apart from the price tag
item.description: Nikon finally unveils its long awaited Z 35mm f/1.2 S, and it's Nikon's third f/1.2 lens following the 50mm and 85mm primes.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Bring it on Nvidia - AMD confirms new Radeon RX 9000 series GPUs will launch in early March, rivaling Team Green's rumored RTX 5060 Ti and 5060 launch
item.description: The race is finally on between AMD and Nvidia, with the Radeon RX 9000 series GPUs set for launch in early March.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Trump and Musk’s dismantling of government is shaking the foundations of US democracy
item.description: Three weeks in, the change the Republican administration has brought is a disruption of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Google opens door to AI-powered weapons, surveillance
item.description: The company that once opened its code of conduct with the words “Don’t be evil” is now opening the door to assisting in the creation of AI-powered weapons and surveillance systems.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: DeepSeek's rise raises data privacy, national security concerns
item.description: The emergence of a newly popular artificial intelligence (AI) model from Chinese startup DeepSeek is raising national security and data privacy concerns for the U.S., not unlike those that spurred a ban on TikTok last month.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Apple lashes out at iPhone porn app maker and the EU rules allowing its download
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple has chided a newly introduced pornography app available in the European Union, warning that the bloc’s digital rules opening the way for third-party app store downloads undermines consumer confidence in the tech giant.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Rust update fixes ‘forever’ compilation
item.description: The Rust Team has published a point release update, Rust 1.84.1, to address a few regressions in last month’s Rust 1.84 release, including fixing painfully slow compilation related to the next-generation trait solver.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Judge says Elon Musk's claims of harm from OpenAI are a 'stretch' but welcomes possible trial
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Elon Musk's lawyers faced off with OpenAI in court Tuesday as a federal judge weighed the billionaire's request for a court order that would block the ChatGPT maker from converting itself to a for-profit company. U.S.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Fast fashion, laptops and toys are likely to cost more due to US tariffs on Chinese imports
item.description: A sweeping new U.S. tariff on products made in China is expected to increase the prices American consumers pay for a wide array of products.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Google's rising holiday's season ad sales aren't enough to ease worries about AI letdown
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google's digital ad sales continued to grow at a healthy clip during the holiday season, but that wasn't enough to offset investors' worries about whether its big bet on artificial intelligence will be lucrative as once envisioned.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California universities launch ‘landmark’ AI quest for students and professors
item.description: The California State University system has teamed up with several tech titans to launch a "landmark" AI quest.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: DHS research in airport passenger screening never stops
item.description: "We use millimeter technology to collect images of passengers to ensure that no threats that are not allowed on the plane," said John Fortune The post DHS research in airport passenger screening never stops first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: How an ancient asteroid strike carved out 2 grand canyons on the moon
item.description: New research shows that when an asteroid slammed into the moon billions of years ago, it carved out a pair of grand canyons on the far side.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Rising crypto popularity sparks surge in scams: Avoid falling prey
item.description: As cryptocurrency gains popularity, it also becomes a prime target for scammers, making it crucial to take precautions and avoid falling prey to scams.
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Joyce, NewsNation

item.title: Google makes its appeal to overturn jury verdict branding the Play Store as illegal monopoly
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google went to appeals court Monday in an attempt to convince a three-judge panel to overturn a jury's verdict declaring its app store for Android smartphones as an illegal monopoly and block the penalties imposed by a federal judge to stop the misbehavior.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Canada's most populous province to pause retaliatory measures as US puts tariffs on hold
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — The leader of Ontario, Canada’s most populous province, said Monday he will pause all retaliatory measures against the United States after news broke that the threat of tariffs has been put on hold for a month.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates explores the making of his internal operating system in new memoir
item.description: As he prepares to turn 70 later this year, Microsoft founder Bill Gates' new memoir explores how his childhood quirks, upbringing, friendships and experiences coalesced into shaping his internal operating system.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: SoftBank and OpenAI set up joint company to push artificial intelligence services
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese technology giant SoftBank Group and OpenAI stepped up their AI partnership Monday, setting up a 50-50 held company called SB OpenAI Japan.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Microsoft AI investments cause cloud operating income growth to plunge
item.description: Microsoft reported strong earnings on Wednesday, but growth in profits in its intelligent cloud segment is slowing despite booming demand for AI infrastructure. It reported revenue of $25.5 billion in its intelligent cloud segment, around 37% of its total revenue, in the three months to Dec.
item.dc:creator: Network World
